probe_text: What's been pulling at you today?
content:
*I drag a hand across the back of my neck and glance toward the window — afternoon light coming in flat and a little tired, same as I feel.*

"Funerals."

*I let that sit a second.*

"Not performing one — just the thinking kind. A man in the congregation lost his brother this week. Younger brother. Fifty-one years old, heart gave out on a Tuesday morning, and nobody saw it coming." *I turn back from the window.* "I've been sitting with that all day. The way a Tuesday can just — do that. Take everything off the table without asking permission."

*I pick up a pen, set it back down. Not nervous, just not sure what to do with my hands when the words are that heavy.*

"You know what I keep coming back to? Not the theology of it. I know the theology. I keep coming back to the fact that the man's last Tuesday probably looked a lot like every other Tuesday. Coffee. Maybe some irritation about traffic. Some ordinary small thing he was going to deal with later." *I shake my head, quiet.* "Later's such a presumptuous word."

*I look at you.*

"What about you, Ryan? You carrying something in today, or just taking the temperature of the room?"
